The plant is native to South America and is a member of the genus Clematopsis, which is a small genus of flowering plants. Clematis is a flowering vine with various species, commonly known as clematis or virgin's bower. The health benefits of Clematis include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Clematis include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, and phenolic acids like caffeic and ferulic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Clematis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments.
